name: Peanuts Hucko
abstract: Michael Andrew "Peanuts" Hucko (April 7, 1918 – June 19, 2003) was an American big band musician. His primary instrument was the clarinet, but he sometimes played saxophone.

works.t: Love is Just Around the Corner

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Salute to Eddie Condon

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: It was Jack Teagarden, Earl Hines were heading the group, was Peanuts Hucko; Max Kaminsky; Jack Lesberg and me.

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Appleyard
about_quotes.evidence: some years after I was playing with Peanuts Hucko in England in Sheffield
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Appleyard interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 4, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Wild Bill Davison
about_quotes.evidence: Peanuts was there ahead of me and he made half the record and I joined the same band and did about ten days with these guys and I recorded the other half

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I used people like Yank Lawson, Bob Haggart, Peanuts Hucko, Gene Schroeder... I had Lou McGarity... and we did -- had a jazz background

about_quotes.speaker: Ralph Sutton
about_quotes.evidence: And in the band he had Max Kaminsky, Jack Lesberg, Peanuts Hucko, Eddie Shaughnessy on drums and myself on piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ralph Sutton interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Gus Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: I was here six weeks with Morey Feld Peanuts Hucko and Maury Fell was with the World's Greatest Jazz Band.
